seoTitle: Flinton vs Florence Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Flinton and Florence Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Flinton vs Florence Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Flinton and Florence Bay.
heading: Flinton vs Florence Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Flinton, Queensland and Florence Bay, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Flinton has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Florence Bay (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Flinton or Florence Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Flinton has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Florence Bay scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Flinton scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Florence Bay scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Flinton or Florence Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Flinton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
